content.title: Example 1 — Logistics (Pseudocode)
content.body: The simplest version: `RoadLogistics` and `SeaLogistics` each override `createTransport()`. The `planDelivery()` method in the base class never changes.

content.title: Logistics — Pseudocode
Example 2 — Cross-Platform Dialog (Java)
content.body: `Dialog.render()` is written once and never changes. Adding `LinuxDialog` requires only creating `LinuxButton` and `LinuxDialog` — nothing else is touched.

content.title: Cross-Platform Dialog
Example 3 — Document Converter (Java)
content.body: Adding `MarkdownExporter` requires exactly two new classes (`MarkdownDocument` + `MarkdownExporter`). The `exportDocument()` business method is never modified.

content.title: Document Converter
Example 4 — Cross-Platform GUI (C++)
content.body: `GUIFramework::renderUI()` delegates button creation to the factory method. `WindowsUI` and `MacUI` each return their platform-specific button.

content.title: Cross-Platform GUI — C++

flashcards.q: In the cross-platform GUI example, how can a subclass change the behavior of `render()` without overriding `render()` itself?
flashcards.a: By overriding `createButton()`. The `render()` method calls `createButton()` internally — by changing what `createButton()` returns, the subclass changes the ingredient that `render()` works with, without touching `render()` itself.

problems.q: **Notification System:** Design a system using Factory Method that supports Email, SMS, and Push notifications. Define all four pattern participants.
problems.a:
**Product:** `Notification` interface with `send(String message)`.
**Concrete Products:** `EmailNotification`, `SMSNotification`, `PushNotification`.
**Creator:** abstract `NotificationCreator` with `createNotification()` factory method and `notify(String message)` that calls it.
**Concrete Creators:** `EmailCreator`, `SMSCreator`, `PushCreator`.

Client only changes which Creator it instantiates. `notify()` never changes.
problems.hint: The notify() method in Creator calls createNotification() then send(). Client only picks which Creator to instantiate.

problems.q: **Extend the Document Converter:** Add a `MarkdownDocument` format. Write only the new code needed — do not modify any existing classes. How many new classes do you need?
problems.hint: You need one Concrete Product and one Concrete Creator. Nothing else changes.
